summary: Setting SKPaint.IsAntialias = true in version 2.88.0-preview.187 triggered GetFont() and UpdateFontEdging(), which lazily allocated a ConcurrentDictionary via OwnedObjects — making SKPaint construction unexpectedly expensive when IsAntialias is set.

stepsToReproduce: Create a new SKPaint with IsAntialias = true
Observe that GetFont() is called, which calls OwnedBy(), which lazily allocates a ConcurrentDictionary

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: Current SKPaint.IsAntialias setter calls sk_compatpaint_set_is_antialias directly without invoking GetFont() or UpdateFontEdging(). The dedicated C API was added to handle this case efficiently.

regression.reason: Reporter explicitly states this was introduced by PR #1802. The IsAntialias code path changed to route through font update logic.

fixStatus.reason: Current SKPaint.cs line 107 shows IsAntialias setter calls SkiaApi.sk_compatpaint_set_is_antialias(Handle, value) directly — no GetFont() call in this path. The dedicated sk_compatpaint_set_is_antialias C API function was added to handle this cleanly.

analysis.summary: Setting SKPaint.IsAntialias triggered a GetFont() → OwnedBy() → OwnedObjects chain that lazily allocated a ConcurrentDictionary in SKObject. The current code addresses this with sk_compatpaint_set_is_antialias which bypasses the font update path entirely.

codeInvestigation.finding: IsAntialias setter now calls SkiaApi.sk_compatpaint_set_is_antialias(Handle, value) directly — no GetFont() or font edging update in the current code path

GetFont() lazily wraps sk_compatpaint_get_font result via OwnedBy(), which accesses OwnedObjects (ConcurrentDictionary). This is still called from many deprecated property accessors but NOT from IsAntialias
OwnedObjects is a lazily-initialized ConcurrentDictionary<IntPtr, SKObject>. First access allocates the dictionary, confirming the allocation concern described in the issue

keySignals.text: Since #1802 setting `IsAntiAlias` causes `UpdateFontEdging()` & `GetFont()` to be called, which might allocate a `ConcurrentDictionary`
keySignals.interpretation: Performance regression introduced by PR #1802 — IsAntialias was routed through font update logic unnecessarily

keySignals.text: This is a fairly significant overhead if we just want to use the SKPaint for drawing antialiased lines/paths
keySignals.interpretation: Reporter uses SKPaint for non-text drawing — font properties are irrelevant, so the font update is wasted work

analysis.rationale: This is a genuine performance regression introduced in PR #1802 where IsAntialias setter triggered GetFont()/UpdateFontEdging() resulting in ConcurrentDictionary allocation. Code inspection of the current codebase shows IsAntialias setter now calls sk_compatpaint_set_is_antialias directly, bypassing the font path. The issue appears fixed in the current version, though no explicit version number can be confirmed due to shallow git history.
workarounds: Cache and reuse SKPaint instances instead of creating new ones each frame
Upgrade to the latest SkiaSharp version which uses sk_compatpaint_set_is_antialias for IsAntialias
nextQuestions: Which version introduced the sk_compatpaint_set_is_antialias fix?
Is there still measurable overhead from ConcurrentDictionary allocation in other SKPaint property setters?

resolution.hypothesis: The dedicated sk_compatpaint_set_is_antialias C API was added to handle the antialias property without going through the font edging update path, fixing the performance regression.

proposals.title: Close as fixed
proposals.description: The current code in SKPaint.cs routes IsAntialias through sk_compatpaint_set_is_antialias, not GetFont(). The reported regression is no longer present.

proposals.title: Workaround: cache SKPaint
proposals.description: Cache SKPaint instances and reset with paint.Reset() instead of creating new ones per frame to avoid any allocation overhead.

resolution.recommendedReason: Current code path no longer calls GetFont() from IsAntialias setter. The dedicated API was added to handle this case.

actionability.reason: The specific code path reported (IsAntialias → UpdateFontEdging → GetFont → ConcurrentDictionary) no longer exists. Current SKPaint.cs uses sk_compatpaint_set_is_antialias which is a direct native call without font overhead.

actions.description: Apply bug, area/SkiaSharp, os/Windows-Classic, tenet/performance labels
Inform reporter that the specific code path has been fixed in current versions
actions.comment: Thanks for reporting this! After investigating the current codebase, the specific performance issue you described (IsAntialias → UpdateFontEdging → GetFont → ConcurrentDictionary allocation) has been addressed. The `SKPaint.IsAntialias` setter now calls `sk_compatpaint_set_is_antialias` directly, bypassing the font update path entirely. Could you verify whether the issue is still present in the latest release (3.x)? If you're still seeing performance overhead, a cached `SKPaint` approach is a good workaround: create the paint once and reuse it, calling `Reset()` when needed.

actions.description: Close as fixed — the regression is no longer present in the current code
